| 2026-04-15 00:00:00 |  |  | Food banks or food stamps (SNAP)
 Finding housing | I’m currently working with Greg S in the electric department to get heat & power turned on at my house. 
 
 I did receive a court document in the mail stating that my lease was terminated. I do have pets, I’m wondering if there is any assistance to get into an apartment and to get a storage unit for my personal items for the time being? 
 
 Additionally i’m wondering about SNAP (food stamps) and if i’d be able to quality for them? 
 
 
 I’m currently operating out of the Motel 6 due to loss of heat & electricity. They said I can stay until the 24th until I have to find a new hotel due to their company policies. 
 
 I’ve had to rent a vehicle through Turo since my vehicle was repossessed on April 1st 2026. 
 Between needing a hotel for my pets & I and a rental car to get to and from work & appointments, my funds are dried out completely. I do have some frozen food left over but not much. 
 
 I lost my job last month and fell very behind, i’m currently working a full time job and will be starting a second full time job within the next couple of weeks. I just need assistance to get back on my feet if it’s at all possible. | Email

| 2026-04-22 00:00:00 |  |  | Paying utility bills | I am a veteran currently receiving rental assistance through Volunteers of America and working with a case manager. I have recently received disconnect notices for both electric and water and am seeking immediate assistance to prevent shutoff.

 We do have near-term income starting—my wife has been accepted as a food vendor at Music on Main, the farmers market, and other events this summer, which will generate income soon. However, we are currently in a short-term gap and need assistance to remain stable.
 
 I am looking for:
 
 Immediate utility assistance (electric and water)
 Any emergency programs to prevent disconnection
 Guidance on additional veteran or family support resources
 
 I am actively working with my case manager and taking steps toward long-term stability, but need help bridging this immediate gap. | Email

| 2026-04-29 00:00:00 |  |  | Paying utility bills
 Food banks or food stamps (SNAP)
 Childcare
 Finding housing
 Health coverage | I am a mother of four children (ages 12, 11, 9, and 7) and have been dealing with long-term, complex chronic health issues for most of their lives. I have very few documented diagnoses including MTHFR and Chronic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (CIRS), along with multiple additional undocumented conditions that have significantly impacted my ability to function day-to-day (including FND- walking and talking are big issues).
 
 Due to my health, I have spent large portions of my children’s lives limited to bed, the couch, or the bath while still being their primary caregiver. My husband works full time, but we have consistently struggled financially due to ongoing housing instability and medical-related limitations.
 
 Over the years, we have moved frequently (over a dozen times), including periods of extreme instability such as living in a trailer that was later totaled and spending extended time living in a tent and moving between campgrounds. We have had to repeatedly give up most of our belongings due to these circumstances.
 
 Despite my husband’s income being slightly above qualification thresholds for many assistance programs, we have not been able to afford medical care, meet insurance deductibles, or access consistent support. I have not received regular medical care in approximately six years due to cost barriers, and we are currently dealing with untreated or under-treated health conditions for both myself and potentially my children.
 
 We are seeking help with:
 - Case management to navigate medical, financial, and family support systems 
 - Access to affordable or sliding-scale medical care and evaluation 
 - Guidance on disability application (SSI/SSDI) 
 - Mental health support for both myself and my children 
 - Any available assistance related to housing stability or financial strain 
 
 We have been navigating this largely alone for many years and are at a point where coordinated support is needed to stabilize our situation. Any guidance or connection to resources would be deeply appreciated. | Email

 Emergency shelter
 Finding housing | I’m looking for a three bedroom home for my two children and myself. My daughter has just gotten out of the hospital and is still recovering from a myriad of operations that doctors performed on her to treat autoimmune issues and issues of bone damage. They have still yet to figure out what is going on with her, and so she is in a very sensitive state health-wise. She wasn’t doing well in Maryland. We came to Bozeman, Montana in hopes that the fresh air here would be better for her. She has started to improve. We ran out of money while looking for a place to live. We need to avoid homeless if at all possible, as that would be very detrimental to my daughter’s recovery. | Email

| 2025-08-23 00:00:00 |  |  | Food banks or food stamps (SNAP)
 Finding housing
 Order of Protection/ Restraining Order | I have to get a restraining order against someone that almost assaulted me. He has been harassing a deputy to get my attention wrongfully. I have footage of him from the GoPro he gave me (after he wrongfully took photos of my rear in a bathing suit that might also be attached to his cloud/ technology and potential footage I don't know about) and has potentially been defaming me and threatening to get a warrant for my arrest and lying to the police. I haven't gotten an order of protection before and I would like legal guidance. The deputy called me multiple times yesterday because he harassed them enough and the deputy showed up at my place of residence without prior notice. He walked by where I was pet sitting last week (I have evidence) and I believe there may be a tracker on my car and need to feel safe. I am looking for different housing options so he cannot show up looking for me and he knows where I live. I pet sit and train dogs for a living and have to hike dogs and I am afraid I will bump into me. He consistently puts me down in texts and emails to the point nobody can keep up with his stories. I think he did this yesterday because the attorney's office is closed on the weekend and he knows how to manipulate people and the system as he talks about how he was the head of the postal service for 20 some years. He says he is going to use my story and publish books as well. I haven't read every single email from him yet because I have to live my life and have my mental health in check though he is trying to stir me up. His wife has a restraining order against him as well I believe. Should I get in touch with the attorney or what is my best bet? When my car was broken into last September downtown, the responding officer brought a ride along dressed in sweats and chewing gum. They did not take any picture evidence and I felt they didn't take me seriously. I handed them blood evidence from a piece of cardboard from whoever broke into my car and they said it may be a year before they can submit it to evidence. Thousands of dollars worth of items were stolen from my car including sensitive tax information, jewelry, my external hard drive with almost all of my pictures, videos, documents, etc. It has been about a year and yet I am being spam called about a GoPro that is not his and he wants the evidence of him almost assaulting me to be off of the record (I believe). He lives up Sypes Canyon area so I try not to hike there and he says his house is valued at a few million dollars. He does stock trading and I'm not sure but I believe since he has my email and phone number that he has been adding me to spam servers. I am not technologically savvy but any pics I deleted of myself he may still have on his cloud and he can still use pictures of me against my will and defame me all along while I am looking for housing and trying to stay afloat with my dog business. He claimed that I stole his phone yet he has it along with multiple pictures of me that he can be using against my will. He has a lot of people's contact information and sends pictures of himself with attractive females to try to get more attractive females in his life as he is divorcing his wife. Please let me know what I can do about this situation! I have to work with a dog today so it is best to text/ email and I have to find out if there is some sort of tracker on my car. | Email

| 2025-08-27 00:00:00 |  |  | Unhoused Persons Living Out of Broken Down Vehicle on Our Street | Hello!
 
 For the past two to three weeks, three men have been spending much of the day in and around a very broken-down Subaru station wagon parked on South 15th in Bozeman, across from where I live.
 
 I am not sure of their situation. They have not threatened anyone’s safety, and while they sometimes gather with others around the vehicle in the afternoons, they are not bothering anyone. I do not know whether they are unhoused or simply using the vehicle as a place to spend time.
 
 I’m reaching out because I am uncertain how best to respond. I do not want to risk confrontation by approaching them directly, but I am concerned that someone may eventually call the police, especially with the new urban camping ordinance in effect. That could make their lives needlessly more difficult if they are in fact unhoused.
 
 Does HRDC have trained outreach workers who could engage with these individuals in a supportive, non-enforcement capacity, and help connect them with any resources they might need?
 
 Apologies in advance is this request is inappropriate or outside of your organization's scope.
 
 Best,
 David | Text message | (406) 600-8815 | david.swedman@gmail.com | David Swedman |  |  |  | By checking this box, you agree to receive SMS messages from HRDC. | 2026-01-12 00:00:00 |  |  | Finding housing | Hello,
 
 My name is Julie Harris. I am reaching out on behalf of my uncle, Denny, whose trailer burned down on New Year’s Eve in Livingston. Since the fire, he has been staying at a local motel, but he is only able to remain there until the 18th and urgently needs another housing option.
 
 We have looked into several available places, but unfortunately none have been affordable for him. I am writing to ask whether Livingston HRDC currently has any emergency or short-term housing options available, or if there are any programs he may qualify for that could help with housing.
 
 I am more than willing to assist my uncle with the application process and provide any documentation needed. Any guidance or assistance you could offer would be greatly appreciated during this difficult time.
 
 Thank you very much for your time and consideration.
 
 Julie Harris | Email

 Rent | I recently lost my job on Jan 2nd, do to my continued battle with Covid. Id been sick since Early November, but wasn't tested for Covid until December 2nd. Because of the loss of employment, ive also lost my insurance. 
 
 It was suggested that I contact HRDC for assistance with my Rent, Food, Utilities, ex, as I am still sick and unable to work.
  
 Also, im seeking assistance in navigating the proper direction to take as far as Work Comp, Unemployment, and/or even an Employment Attorney due to circumstances with my termination, and prior while I was unable to work. 
 
 Due to the fact that im still sick (Constant coughing, exhausted, body aches, Etc) Im requesting to speak to someone over the phone rather than in your office. Im sure you understand, I don't want to get anyone else sick, nor do I want to get sicker from anything do to my compromised immune system. If at all possible, can this call be in the afternoon, as my sleep schedule has been very irregular? I do have an appointment at 5pm, Wednesday Jan 14th, and will be unavailable.
 
 Thank you,

| 2025-07-10 00:00:00 |  |  | Need Information | Hi, 
 
 The basics, I am 77 and disabled (neurological back problems from long ago injuries make it very difficult for me to walk). My wife and I were just barely getting by in our house on our paired social security (totalled about $2300/month). 
 
 However, my partner, wife, best friend of 54 years died last month, and about 1/2 of my income disappeared. I am in a world of hurt financially now as well as physically, and psychogically. 
 
 I can't cover my necessary payments (mortgage, electricity, propane, house and car insurance). I am effectively unemployable (too old, too immobile, and too overqualified educationally (Ph. D. in marine ecology = can't get tutoring jobs, etc.) 
 
 So...I was told by my wife's hospice co-ordinator I should contact you folks to see if you had advice/help.... etc.
 
 Name: Ronald Shimek, 210 South Ordway St., Wilsall, MT. Mailing address: P.O. Box 4. Phone (Landline only -so no SMS texts).
 
 Cheers, Ron | Email
 Phone call | (406) 578-9804 | ronaldlshimek@gmail.com | Ronald L Shimek |  |  |  |  | https://thehrdc.org/ |  | https://app.jotform.com/250797867241166 | Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/138.0.0.0 Safari/537.36 | English | 2025-07-10 15:59:00 | Park |
| 2025-07-14 00:00:00 |  |  | Finding housing | I work two jobs but am struggling immensly with debt payments. I am in a program to pay my debts, but my expenses including these debt payements take almost all my income. I am often negative on my finances. I have moved 6 times in the past year around Bozeman and Belgrade areas couch surfing because I have not been able to afford rent. I finally am renting from someone on the outskirts of Belgrade, but the place is not ideal and I am struggling even more to make ends meet. I am tired of living in poor conditions and in non safe environments. I can't go back to my parents (history of alcoholism, famlily trauma). I have no other family here. I am asking for help because I do not qualify for any programs due to my income (since my debts are not considered). Is there any help out there to get something safe and affordable? I feel like I've exhausted all my resources and would appreciate any consideration for affordable housing. | Email

 Emergency shelter
 Finding housing
 Transitional living, support programs, help with budgeting and financail management | Urgent Housing Help Needed – Family Shelter or Transitional Housing
 
 
 I’m reaching out in hopes of finding help. I was recently evicted and am now homeless with my three young children.
 
 I’m currently in the hiring process for a Radiologic Technologist position at Bozeman Health, with a start date in July. I’ve worked hard to get to this point and finally have a real opportunity to get us back on track—but I’m terrified that not having stable housing right now could cause it to fall through.
 
 If there are any emergency family shelters, transitional housing options, or anything available through HRDC that could help us get through this critical time, I would truly appreciate it.
 
 Thank you for taking the time to read this. I really hope to hear | Email
 Phone call

 If you're unable to reach me by phone, please feel free to send a text message. My phone was recently having issues with incoming calls, but it’s been checked out and appears to be working again.  I’m Daniel from the USA, and I’ve been helping businesses around the world with professional web and app design services since 2012.

| 2025-06-24 00:00:00 |  |  | Transportation
 Finding housing | Hi there,
 
 I’m reaching out in hopes of helping my sister find the support she needs to live independently in the Belgrade/Bozeman area. She’s spent much of her life unstably housed and moved here to be closer to me for support after our mother passed away. While she is capable in many ways, she struggles with some day-to-day tasks such as budgeting, maintaining long-term employment, and transportation, as she doesn’t have a driver’s license. She also faces some mental and physical health challenges that make consistency difficult.
 She’s currently on Social Security amd has been on Social Security Disability in the past and is renting a room month-to-month, which is a good first step. However, long-term stable housing is essential for her continued progress. She’s not eligible for REACH or group home support, as her disability doesn’t meet the required threshold, nor is she old enough for senior housing.
 I’ve done my best to support her emotionally and financially, but I no longer have the capacity to do so at the level she needs. I remain nearby and willing to help, but I’m unable to provide full-time support with transportation, finances, or daily routines.
 She has mentioned being on the HRDC housing list and has had conversations about budgeting, but follow-through and communication can be inconsistent. I’d like to:
 • Understand where she currently stands on the housing waitlist and what documents or steps are needed next
 • Help coordinate any classes or resources around budgeting and balancing work with her SS income and if her getting back on SDDI would benefit or hurt her opportunities to work as she really want to
 • Explore transportation options to help her get to and from work or appointments
 
 I’m happy to have her contact you to provide consent for me to be involved. My goal is to help her build stability and avoid the risk of homelessness again.
 
 Thank you so much for any guidance or resources you can provide. I truly want to see her live a safe, healthy, and fulfilling life—but I know I can’t do it alone and we have no other family member that are able to assist either.
 
 Warmly,
 Andrea | Email
 Phone call
 Text message | (406) 552-3421 | taylor.andreac@gmail.com | Andrea |  |  |  | By checking this box, you agree to receive SMS messages from HRDC. You can reply stop to opt-out at any time. Reply Help for customer care at 406-587-4486. Messages and data rates may apply. Message frequency will vary. You can verify our Privacy policy and Terms & Conditions on the following sites: thehrdc.org/privacy-policy and thehrdc.org/terms-and-conditions. |  |  |  |  | English | 2025-06-24 06:43:00 | Gallatin |
| 2025-06-25 00:00:00 |  |  | Dental emergency/surgery | Good Morning,
  I am seeking help for my dental emergencies. I currently am on MT adult Medicaid, however because of their guidelines and cap on bi annual dental funds I feel hopeless. I have visited the Big Sky ER twice since 2023 when I moved to Big Sky from the flathead valley to work. 
 I have severe failing dental health. I visited a dentist in Bozeman and after referral the oral surgeon. I had a job a loved in Big Sky in hospitality but was let go last summer and I feel was directly related to my visible dental problems. I have become depressed , desperate and feel this can not be happening. My dentist ans oral surgeon agree it is time for dentures. However Medicaid adult (43 yrs not senior) has a 1300.00 cap that resets in the summer. Therefore the oral surgeon can extract 3 to 4 teeth, after July can extract 3 to 4 more. Then I have to wait an entire year with little to no teeth but unable to get get or wear dentures. 
 I have pleaded with health and human services , and told if deemed medically necessary it would be covered. I reached out to the doctor I was seen by in the ER both times however it isn’t now a year later and am still struggling to eat, to look for a new job in hospitality , I currently do night time cleaning work, I struggle to even leave my room. 
 My dream, I am manifesting with every email or time my story is shared is to find financial sponsorship for all on 4 implant surgery through bionic smile. It would be life changing, possible life saving. I would have something I have never had, a confident smile and a healthy mouth. I want so badly to work in my community again, social interaction and relationships directly impact my overall health and well being. I want to volunteer in my community. All of that has been out of reach for fear of embarrassment and judgement especially in Big Sky. 
 Please do not hesitate with any questions, information or direction your organization can direct me in. Thank you for your time. | Email

| 2025-06-06 00:00:00 |  |  | Paying utility bills
 Emergency rent help | I was unemployed last year due to being laid off (company closed), and struggled to find decent employment for months. I did eventually get a new job and worked there for several months. However, I was ONCE AGAIN laid off (again not my fault; it was equipment failure/lack of available work), and have been looking for work since day one of getting that text. (Yes, they laid me off rather unprofessionally over text and with zero prior notice.)
 I am now in very dire straights because rent is due and I cannot pay. I already exhausted all my chances with the owner of my trailer court. I am all caught up from past months, so I don't owe any back rent, but he won't allow me to be late with payments anymore without incurring consequences. 
 I can't apply for new unemployment for another few days. Even when I do apply, I won't get paid for at least a week, and it won't make up for the several weeks of pay that I'm missing. It also probably won't be enough to fully cover the total rent cost ($650), and it doesn't help that I also have credit cards and utilities to pay. 
 In short, I'm basically broke (less than $300 in my bank account), and I need to pay my rent NOW or risk being evicted and becoming homeless. I asked for help from "friends", but no one is willing to lend me any money. I just need enough to pay rent for this month, and hopefully I will be working again within a week or so. I have a couple of prospects on the table. But even if I started tomorrow, I wouldn't get paid for several weeks. 
 
 I could give my landlord the check today, but as soon as he cashes it, my bank account is going in the red. And there is a $30+ fee for every transaction/day while the account is overdrawn. Which adds up really fast. Putting me even further in the hole.
 As I said, I just need enough cash to pay my lot rent for this month; after that, I should be regularly working again and can make up for lost time. Please help. I'm doing my best to get back on my feet, but it's hard when I keep getting repeatedly knocked down. | Email

 Grants for homesteading or communal agricultural production | Moved here with my disabled father Dan who's a marine corps veteran. We have a rental truck full of stuff and no storage to put it in. Our gas gauge on our personal vehicle so we have no idea how much gas is in it. We moved here from Colorado because my father couldn't get the medical assistance or any other type of assistance he needed there. We're sitting in the parking lot at the truck stop and gas station in three forks Sleeping in our vehicle and running it to stay warm. Our whole move went really badly it took way longer and used up way more gas than anticipated. We have some canned and dried food and a little water but not much more. And we only have a little money left most of which needs to go to trying to get a storage so we can return the rental truck. I've already began looking for work but I have to temper that with not knowing how much gas is left in my tank. Our vehicle is also 20+ years old and in pretty bad shape due to it already being in disrepair when we got it and not having the money for mechanics. We have a lot going on right now and not much going well. | Email

 Transportation | grace helped me fill out my food stamps and leapp application on january 21 - grace took copies of it and was supposed to submit it she did not submit the application so my electricity was turned off on march 25 which left me without electricity or water. i ran out of water on april 10 and was then committed to mt state hospital - i am about to be released / discharged, and need my electricity turned on. i am asking that my january 21 2026 application that "grace" helped me fillout be processed by the hrdc retroactively as it was filled out and submitted by me with and to grace, at the hrdc gallatin county food bank on january 21 2026... if it had been submitted by grace at that time it would have been timely- during leapp application deadlines (by april 30) and it would have been approved- and my electricity would not have been interupted. please contact justin felde careplanner at mt state hospital at 406 - 693 - 7045 before 4pm today monday may 18, 2026 to discuss | Email

| 2025-09-24 00:00:00 |  |  | Finding housing | Hi,
 
 I'm a graduate student at MSU looking for some help finding housing. I have some questions about income-restricted housing. 
 
 I work part-time. I made $30,000 last year and am on track to make about the same this year so it seems like I would quality for income restricted housing, but I have two questions.
 
 1) I'm only taking 6 credits a semester this fall and spring, when I'll be graduating. MSU defines full time student status for grad students as 9 or more a semester, so I'm assuming I won't be ineligible for income restricted housing on that basis. But I wanted to know if these apartments have a different definition for full time student status.
 
 2) After I graduate in May, I'll be looking for full time jobs. How does it work if I start making more than the income limit for the apartment I'm in?
 
 Thank you! | Email | (865) 307-2321 | lexjharold@gmail.com | Lex Harold |  |  |  | By checking this box, you agree to receive SMS messages from HRDC. You can reply stop to opt-out at any time. I live in an RV with nowhere to park anymore. It has no plates or registration. My car broke down so I have to drive it to work and risk having my home taken from me every time I drive it, or park somewhere I'm not supposed to. This is going to be my 3rd winter doing this, and I can't do it anymore. I have no addiction/alcohol issues and don't use drugs or drink. I have no criminal record. I was approved at The Beaumont, but that was 1 month BEFORE I was on the Housing Choice waitlust. My # is in the mid-200's on the waitlist. Please help me. Please. | 2025-09-05 00:00:00 |  |  | Needing a person who can be a social worker/ advocate for a senior inn our program. | Dear HRDC,
 I am reaching out regarding a senior, MaryBeth, who currently resides at Darlington Manor. She has expressed that she does not want to remain living there and would do anything to find alternative housing. Unfortunately, her situation is quite challenging; her car is not working, she has no family connections, and only a very limited support network of friends.
 
 I have previously tried to help her explore housing options, but reached a dead end. I was wondering if there are any case workers who might be able to work with MaryBeth to explore secure housing, food assistance, and additional resources. She would greatly benefit from having someone to advocate for her and guide her through available programs.
 
 MaryBeth is 78 years old and has some cognitive challenges related to brain cancer she experienced years ago. While she does have a Befriender volunteer who provides meaningful support, I believe she would significantly benefit from a professional with knowledge of senior resources and case management experience.
 
 Thank you for considering this request. Please let me know if there is a way to connect her with a case worker or next steps we should take.
 
 Warmly,
 
 Silvia Schmitz, Program Manager with Befrienders. | Email

| 2025-09-07 00:00:00 |  |  | Emergency shelter
 Finding housing
 Abuse | So I was residing at Off The Streets and I had a relationship with someone my same gender there and it bothered our roommate. I cuddled her on my last night with her because she was going to a paid room. The next day I get thrown out and when I return after having to rent out a hotel for almost a week I come back and ask and I get accused of being a drug pusher and without giving me a chance to say anything I was thrown out due to this false rumor. Dena and Austin know their residents have bed bugs and refuses to take care of it and expects the residents to get over it. There is also a cockroach infestation that was just as equally been ignored. Right now, I feel hopeless because I lost my job and have no reliability. I foolishly bought a bus pass somewhere with my last bit of money and after realizing the town I paid to go to Great Falls was not a safe place for people of color (as I biracial person). What happened to me wasn't right and I'm not an awful person. The resident Zack at Off The Streets has been pushing drugs himself on people and Dena doesn't care and chooses his word over mine despite smelling of pot. Please if I can get any assistance or action towards the unsafe living environment because what happened to me was not okay and the other residents who are forced to live there in that situation is not okay.
 
 (I'm residing in billings MT, Yellowstone county. There wasn't an option for it so I chose a different county) | Email
